1342	
1343	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
1344	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.

1352	        )
1353	    pass
1354

1356	    arguments["model"] = self
1357	    del arguments["self"]
1358	    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1359	    for _ in range(3):
1360	        gc.collect()

1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto

1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1821	
1822	    arguments = dict(locals())
1823	    arguments["model"]        = self
1824	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
1825	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
1827	    del arguments["self"]

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out
190	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,

376	    )

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O

470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	
3	"""
4	🦥 Starter Script for Fine-Tuning FastLanguageModel with Unsloth
5	
6	This script is designed as a starting point for fine-tuning your models using unsloth.
7	It includes configurable options for model loading, PEFT parameters, training arguments, 
8	and model saving/pushing functionalities.
9	
10	You will likely want to customize this script to suit your specific use case 
11	and requirements.
12	
13	Here are a few suggestions for customization:
14	    - Modify the dataset loading and preprocessing steps to match your data.
15	    - Customize the model saving and pushing configurations.
16	
17	Usage: (most of the options have valid default values this is an extended example for demonstration purposes)
18	    python unsloth-cli.py --model_name "unsloth/llama-3-8b" --max_seq_length 8192 --dtype None --load_in_4bit \
19	    --r 64 --lora_alpha 32 --lora_dropout 0.1 --bias "none" --use_gradient_checkpointing "unsloth" \
20	    --random_state 3407 --use_rslora --per_device_train_batch_size 4 --gradient_accumulation_steps 8 \
21	    --warmup_steps 5 --max_steps 400 --learning_rate 2e-6 --logging_steps 1 --optim "adamw_8bit" \
22	    --weight_decay 0.005 --lr_scheduler_type "linear" --seed 3407 --output_dir "outputs" \
23	    --report_to "tensorboard" --save_model --save_path "model" --quantization_method "f16" \
24	    --push_model --hub_path "hf/model" --hub_token "your_hf_token"
25	
26	To see a full list of configurable options, use:
27	    python unsloth-cli.py --help
28	
29	Happy fine-tuning!
30	"""
31	
32	import argparse
33	import os
34	
35	
36	def run(args):
37	    import torch
38	    from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
39	    from datasets import load_dataset
40	    from transformers.utils import strtobool
41	    from trl import SFTTrainer, SFTConfig
42	    from transformers import TrainingArguments
43	    from unsloth import is_bfloat16_supported
44	    import logging
45	    logging.getLogger('hf-to-gguf').setLevel(logging.WARNING)
46	
47	    # Load model and tokenizer
48	    model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
49	        model_name=args.model_name,
50	        max_seq_length=args.max_seq_length,
51	        dtype=args.dtype,
52	        load_in_4bit=args.load_in_4bit,
53	    )
54	
55	    # Configure PEFT model
56	    model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
57	        model,
58	        r=args.r,
59	        target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
60	                        "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
61	        lora_alpha=args.lora_alpha,
62	        lora_dropout=args.lora_dropout,
63	        bias=args.bias,
64	        use_gradient_checkpointing=args.use_gradient_checkpointing,
65	        random_state=args.random_state,
66	        use_rslora=args.use_rslora,
67	        loftq_config=args.loftq_config,
68	    )
69	
70	    alpaca_prompt = """Below is an instruction that describes a task, paired with an input that provides further context. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.
71	
72	    ### Instruction:
73	    {}
74	
75	    ### Input:
76	    {}
77	
78	    ### Response:
79	    {}"""
80	
81	    EOS_TOKEN = tokenizer.eos_token  # Must add EOS_TOKEN
82	    def formatting_prompts_func(examples):
83	        instructions = examples["instruction"]
84	        inputs       = examples["input"]
85	        outputs      = examples["output"]
86	        texts = []
87	        for instruction, input, output in zip(instructions, inputs, outputs):
88	            text = alpaca_prompt.format(instruction, input, output) + EOS_TOKEN
89	            texts.append(text)
90	        return {"text": texts}
91	
92	    use_modelscope = strtobool(os.environ.get('UNSLOTH_USE_MODELSCOPE', 'False'))
93	    if use_modelscope:
94	        from modelscope import MsDataset
95	        dataset = MsDataset.load(args.dataset, split="train")
96	    else:
97	        # Load and format dataset
98	        dataset = load_dataset(args.dataset, split="train")
99	    dataset = dataset.map(formatting_prompts_func, batched=True)
100	    print("Data is formatted and ready!")
101	
102	    # Configure training arguments
103	    training_args = SFTConfig(
104	        per_device_train_batch_size=args.per_device_train_batch_size,
105	        gradient_accumulation_steps=args.gradient_accumulation_steps,
106	        warmup_steps=args.warmup_steps,
107	        max_steps=args.max_steps,
108	        learning_rate=args.learning_rate,
109	        fp16=not is_bfloat16_supported(),
110	        bf16=is_bfloat16_supported(),
111	        logging_steps=args.logging_steps,
112	        optim=args.optim,
113	        weight_decay=args.weight_decay,
114	        lr_scheduler_type=args.lr_scheduler_type,
115	        seed=args.seed,
116	        output_dir=args.output_dir,
117	        report_to=args.report_to,
118	        max_length=args.max_seq_length,
119	        dataset_num_proc=2,
120	        packing=False,
121	    )
122	
123	    # Initialize trainer
124	    trainer = SFTTrainer(
125	        model=model,
126	        processing_class=tokenizer,
127	        train_dataset=dataset,
128	        args=training_args,
129	    )
130	
131	    # Train model
132	    trainer_stats = trainer.train()
133	
134	    # Save model
135	    if args.save_model:
136	        # if args.quantization_method is a list, we will save the model for each quantization method
137	        if args.save_gguf:
138	            if isinstance(args.quantization, list):
139	                for quantization_method in args.quantization:
140	                    print(f"Saving model with quantization method: {quantization_method}")
141	                    model.save_pretrained_gguf(
142	                        args.save_path,
143	                        tokenizer,
144	                        quantization_method=quantization_method,
145	                    )
146	                    if args.push_model:
147	                        model.push_to_hub_gguf(
148	                            hub_path=args.hub_path,
149	                            hub_token=args.hub_token,
150	                            quantization_method=quantization_method,
151	                        )
152	            else:
153	                print(f"Saving model with quantization method: {args.quantization}")
154	                model.save_pretrained_gguf(args.save_path, tokenizer, quantization_method=args.quantization)
155	                if args.push_model:
156	                    model.push_to_hub_gguf(
157	                        hub_path=args.hub_path,
158	                        hub_token=args.hub_token,
159	                        quantization_method=quantization_method,
160	                    )
161	        else:
162	            model.save_pretrained_merged(args.save_path, tokenizer, args.save_method)
163	            if args.push_model:
164	                model.push_to_hub_merged(args.save_path, tokenizer, args.hub_token)
165	    else:
166	        print("Warning: The model is not saved!")

70	class UnslothTrainingArguments(TrainingArguments):

76	
77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass
119	
120	
121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass
137	pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass

95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \

     def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
         self,
         save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
         tokenizer=None,
         save_method: str = "merged_16bit",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]

         state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,

         max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of the structure and key components:

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY LAYOUT

**Root directory** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/`):
- `unsloth/` - Main package directory
- `tests/` - Test suite
- `pyproject.toml` - Project configuration
- `README.md` - Documentation
- `unsloth-cli.py` - CLI entry point
- `.github/` - GitHub workflows
- `images/` - Documentation images

**unsloth/ subdirectories**:
- `models/` - Model implementations and loading logic
- `kernels/` - Optimized CUDA/Triton kernels for LoRA operations
- `registry/` - Model registry and configuration
- `dataprep/` - Data preparation utilities
- `utils/` - Utility functions
- Core files: `__init__.py`, `save.py`, `chat_templates.py`, `tokenizer_utils.py`, `trainer.py`, `import_fixes.py`

---

### 2. PRIMARY MODEL CLASSES (Main User Interface)

The primary model classes are defined in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/models/loader.py`:

**FastLanguageModel** (lines 107-525)
- Main user-facing class for language model loading
- Inherits from `FastLlamaModel`
- Primary method: `from_pretrained()` - loads and patches models with LoRA support
- Handles 4-bit, 8-bit, and 16-bit quantization loading
- Supports fast inference via vLLM

**FastModel** (lines 526-994)
- Base class for all model variants
- Inherits from `FastBaseModel`
- Full finetuning support without LoRA

**FastVisionModel** (lines 995-997)
- Vision-specific model handling
- Inherits from `FastModel`

**FastTextModel** (lines 998+)
- Text-specific model handling
- Inherits from `FastModel`

**Supporting Model Classes** (in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/models/`):
- `FastLlamaModel` (llama.py) - Base implementation for Llama architectures
- `FastMistralModel` (mistral.py)
- `FastGemmaModel` (gemma.py)
- `FastGemma2Model` (gemma2.py)
- `FastQwen2Model` (qwen2.py)
- `FastQwen3Model` (qwen3.py)
- `FastQwen3MoeModel` (qwen3_moe.py)
- `FastGraniteModel` (granite.py)
- `FastCohereModel` (cohere.py)
- `FastFalconH1Model` (falcon_h1.py)

---

### 3. LORA ADAPTER REPRESENTATION & CONFIGURATION

**LoRA Configuration Creation** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/models/llama.py`, lines 2266-2617):

The `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` method is responsible for creating LoRA adapters:

```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r                   = 16,  # LoRA rank
    target_modules      = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                           "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha          = 16,  # Scaling factor
    lora_dropout        = 0.0,
    bias                = "none",
    layers_to_transform = None,
    layers_pattern      = None,
    use_gradient_checkpointing = "unsloth",
    random_state        = 3407,
    max_seq_length      = 2048,
    use_rslora          = False,  # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save     = None,  # Modules to train fully (embed_tokens, lm_head)
    init_lora_weights   = True,
    loftq_config        = {},  # LoFTQ initialization
    **kwargs,
):
```

**LoRA Arguments Dictionary** (lines 2598-2617):
```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,
    target_modules      = final_modules,
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,
    bias                = bias,
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,
    loftq_config        = loftq_config,
    use_rslora          = use_rslora,
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,
    **kwargs,
)
lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)  # From PEFT library
```

**LoRA Parameter Access** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`, lines 235-257):

The `get_lora_parameters_bias()` function extracts LoRA matrices from layers:
```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default",))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    return (
        W,                              # Base weight matrix
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),  # Quantization state (for 4-bit)
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,    # LoRA A matrix (input projection)
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,    # LoRA B matrix (output projection)
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # Scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)
        base_layer.bias,
    )
```

**Supported Target Modules** (lines 2491-2492):
```python
accepted_modules = frozenset((
    "q_proj",    # Query projection in attention
    "k_proj",    # Key projection in attention
    "v_proj",    # Value projection in attention
    "o_proj",    # Output projection in attention
    "gate_proj", # Gate in SwiGLU/MLP
    "up_proj",   # Up projection in MLP
    "down_proj", # Down projection in MLP
))
```

---

### 4. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS

**Main Save Function** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/save.py`, lines 205-762):

```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory,
    save_method = "lora",  # Options: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
    push_to_hub = False,
    token = None,
    is_main_process = True,
    state_dict = None,
    save_function = torch.save,
    max_shard_size = "5GB",
    safe_serialization = True,
    variant = None,
    save_peft_format = True,
    # ... more push_to_hub args
)
```

**Save Methods**:
- **"lora"**: Saves only LoRA adapters (adapter_config.json, adapter_model.bin)
- **"merged_16bit"**: Merges LoRA weights into base model weights and saves as float16 (for GGUF conversion)
- **"merged_4bit"**: Merges LoRA weights and saves as 4-bit quantized model (for inference)

**LoRA Merging** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/save.py`, lines 159-187):

The `_merge_lora()` function merges LoRA matrices into the base weight:
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            # Merge: W = W + s * A^T @ B^T
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

**Patched Save Methods** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/save.py`, lines 2569-2668):

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # Wraps model methods:
    model.push_to_hub               # Patched to unsloth_push_to_hub
    model.save_pretrained           # Delegates to model.save_pretrained()
    model.push_to_hub_merged        # Merges LoRA and pushes merged weights
    model.save_pretrained_merged    # Merges LoRA and saves locally
    model.push_to_hub_gguf          # Converts to GGUF and pushes
    model.save_pretrained_gguf      # Converts to GGUF locally
    model.save_pretrained_torchao   # Quantizes with TorchAO
    model.push_to_hub_ggml          # Converts LoRA to GGML format
    model.save_pretrained_ggml      # Converts LoRA to GGML locally
```

---

### 5. PACKAGE EXPORTS & PUBLIC API

**Main Package Exports** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/__init__.py`, lines 261-270):

```python
from .models import *  # All model classes
from .models import __version__  # Version info
from .save import *  # Save functions
from .chat_templates import *  # Chat template utilities
from .tokenizer_utils import *  # Tokenizer utilities
from .trainer import *  # Training utilities
```

**Models Exported** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/models/__init__.py`, lines 15-29):

```python
FastLlamaModel, FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel, FastModel
FastMistralModel, FastQwen2Model, FastQwen3Model, FastQwen3MoeModel
FastGraniteModel, FastFalconH1Model
PatchDPOTrainer, PatchKTOTrainer
is_bfloat16_supported, is_vLLM_available, __version__
PatchFastRL, vLLMSamplingParams
```

**Save Functions Exported** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/save.py`, lines 48-54):

```python
print_quantization_methods
unsloth_save_model
save_to_gguf
patch_saving_functions
create_huggingface_repo
```

---

### 6. KEY KERNEL FUNCTIONS FOR LORA

**Fast LoRA Kernels** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`):

Exported functions:
- `get_lora_parameters()` - Extracts LoRA A and B matrices from a layer
- `get_lora_parameters_bias()` - Extracts LoRA matrices plus bias
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` - Applies LoRA to SwiGLU MLP layers
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()` - Applies LoRA to GeGLU MLP (exact)
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_approx()` - Applies LoRA to GeGLU MLP (approximate)
- `apply_lora_qkv()` - Applies LoRA to query/key/value projections
- `apply_lora_o()` - Applies LoRA to output projection
- `fast_lora_forward()` - Fast forward pass with LoRA

**Utils Kernels** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`):

- `fast_dequantize()` - Dequantizes 4-bit weights using bitsandbytes
- `fast_gemv()` - Fast general matrix-vector multiplication
- `matmul_lora()` - Efficient matrix multiplication incorporating LoRA matrices
- `_maybe_fake_quantize_activations()` - QAT support for activations

---

### 7. PEFT MODEL PATCHING

**Patching Process** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/models/llama.py`, lines 2732-2951):

```python
def patch_peft_model(model, use_gradient_checkpointing = "unsloth"):
    # 1. Determines model type and selects appropriate LoRA application function
    # 2. Calls prepare_model_for_kbit_training()
    # 3. Patches PEFT config (fixes loftq_config)
    # 4. Iterates through model layers and applies:
    #    - LoRA to MLP layers (gate, up, down projections)

**FastLanguageModel** (lines 107-525)
- Main user-facing class for language model loading
- Inherits from `FastLlamaModel`
- Primary method: `from_pretrained()` - loads and patches models with LoRA support
- Handles 4-bit, 8-bit, and 16-bit quantization loading
- Supports fast inference via vLLM

**FastModel** (lines 526-994)
- Base class for all model variants
- Inherits from `FastBaseModel`
- Full finetuning support without LoRA

**FastVisionModel** (lines 995-997)

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default",))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    return (
        W,                              # Base weight matrix
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),  # Quantization state (for 4-bit)
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,    # LoRA A matrix (input projection)
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,    # LoRA B matrix (output projection)
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # Scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)
        base_layer.bias,
    )
```

- **"merged_4bit"**: Merges LoRA weights and saves as 4-bit quantized model (for inference)

**LoRA Merging** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/save.py`, lines 159-187):

The `_merge_lora()` function merges LoRA matrices into the base weight:
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            # Merge: W = W + s * A^T @ B^T
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

## Comprehensive LoRA Adapter Weights Structure Analysis - Unsloth Codebase

Based on my thorough search of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how LoRA adapter weights are structured and accessed:

---

### 1. LoRA Matrix Storage and Access

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

**Lines 190-232** - `get_lora_parameters()` function:
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Check if adapters are disabled
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    # Get active adapter
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA matrices - Key structure
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dict access with adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]      # Dict access with adapter name
    
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                  # Linear layer weight (matrix)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight                  # Linear layer weight (matrix)
    
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Lines 235-257** - `get_lora_parameters_bias()` function:
```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Returns 6-tuple including bias
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,      # Access via dict
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,      # Access via dict
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
```

**Key Data Structure:**
- `proj.lora_A` - Dictionary where keys are adapter names (e.g., "default")
- `proj.lora_B` - Dictionary where keys are adapter names
- `proj.scaling` - Dictionary mapping adapter names to scaling factors
- `proj.lora_dropout` - Dictionary mapping adapter names to dropout modules
- Each value in `lora_A[adapter_name]` is a Linear module with `.weight` attribute

---

### 2. LoRA Configuration Storage and Access

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/models/llama.py`

### 7. LoRA Computation in Fast Forward

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

**Lines 734-757** - Matrix Multiplication with LoRA:
```python
def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):
    """
    Compute: X @ W + (X @ A) @ B * s
    where:
    - X: input tensor
    - W: base weight matrix
    - A, B: LoRA matrices
    - s: scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)
    """
    dtype = X.dtype
    W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
    
    out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
    
    if A is not None:
        # LoRA computation: (X @ A) @ B.T * s
        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
        XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
        out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha = s)
    
    return out
```

**Lines 691-731** - Fast Linear Forward with LoRA:
```python
def fast_linear_forward(proj, X, temp_lora = None, out = None):
    W, W_quant, lora_A, lora_B, lora_S, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)
    
    # Compute base: X @ W.T
    out = torch_matmul(X, W.t(), out = out)
    
    # Add LoRA: (X @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T * lora_S
    if lora_A is not None:
        temp_lora = torch_mv(lora_A, X, out = temp_lora)      # X @ lora_A
        out.addmv_(lora_B, temp_lora, alpha = lora_S)         # + result @ lora_B.T * s
    
    if bias is not None:
        out += bias
    
    return out
```

**LoRA Adapter Weight Structure in Unsloth:**

1. **Storage:** Module-level dictionaries indexed by adapter name
   - `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` → Linear(in_features=out_dim, out_features=rank)
   - `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` → Linear(in_features=rank, out_features=out_dim)
   - `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` → float (lora_alpha / rank)

2. **Configuration:** PEFT LoraConfig stored in model
   - `model.peft_config[adapter_name]` → LoraConfig instance
   - Contains: r, lora_alpha, target_modules, bias, lora_dropout, etc.

3. **Access Patterns:** 
   - Via `model.named_parameters()` with pattern matching for "lora_A" and "lora_B"
   - Via `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` for matrix access
   - Via `model.peft_config[adapter_name].r` for config access

4. **Iteration:** Loop over `self.active_adapters` list for multi-adapter scenarios

5. **Computation:** `output = base_output + (input @ lora_A) @ lora_B.T * scaling`

## Comprehensive LoRA Adapter Weights Structure Analysis - Unsloth Codebase

Based on my thorough search of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how LoRA adapter weights are structured and accessed:

---

### 1. LoRA Matrix Storage and Access

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505631539-gul7hz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

**Lines 190-232** - `get_lora_parameters()` function:
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Check if adapters are disabled
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    # Get active adapter
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA matrices - Key structure
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dict access with adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]      # Dict access with adapter name
    
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                  # Linear layer weight (matrix)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight                  # Linear layer weight (matrix)
    
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Lines 235-257** - `get_lora_parameters_bias()` function:
```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Returns 6-tuple including bias
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,      # Access via dict
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,      # Access via dict
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
```

**Key Data Structure:**
- `proj.lora_A` - Dictionary where keys are adapter names (e.g., "default")
- `proj.lora_B` - Dictionary where keys are adapter names
- `proj.scaling` - Dictionary mapping adapter names to scaling factors
- `proj.lora_dropout` - Dictionary mapping adapter names to dropout modules
- Each value in `lora_A[adapter_name]` is a Linear module with `.weight` attribute

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )

def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",

def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

Design an implementation plan for adding an `export_to_onnx` method to the unsloth library that exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

## Context from codebase exploration

### File: `unsloth/save.py`
This is the main file where all save/export methods live. Key structure:
- **Lines 1-54**: Imports, `__all__` exports list (`print_quantization_methods`, `unsloth_save_model`, `save_to_gguf`, `patch_saving_functions`, `create_huggingface_repo`)
- **Lines 159-187**: `_merge_lora(layer, name)` - merges LoRA into base weights using `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
- **Lines 205-760**: `unsloth_save_model()` - core save function (save_method: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit")
- **Lines 1321-1362**: `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(self, save_directory, ...)` - instance method pattern
- **Lines 2129-2140**: `save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory)` - saves LoRA adapters
- **Lines 2211-2262**: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(self, save_directory, tokenizer, ...)` - converts LoRA to GGML format (good pattern to follow)
- **Lines 2564-2566**: `not_implemented_save()` - placeholder
- **Lines 2569-2678**: `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)` - registers methods on model instances via `types.MethodType`. For non-vision models with config (lines 2658-2668), it registers: `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_gguf`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `save_pretrained_torchao`, `push_to_hub_ggml`, `save_pretrained_ggml`

### LoRA weight access patterns
- `model.peft_config["default"]` -> LoraConfig with `.r`, `.lora_alpha`, `.target_modules`
- `model.named_parameters()` with filter for "lora_A" and "lora_B" in name
- Individual layer: `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight`, `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight`, `proj.scaling[adapter_name]`
- Test utility at `tests/utils/data_utils.py:132`: `get_peft_weights(model)` does `{name: param for name, param in model.named_parameters() if is_lora_weight(name)}`
- State dict naming: `base_model.model.model.layers.{i}.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.default.weight`

### Existing method pattern (e.g., GGML save locally, lines 2211-2262):
```python
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str,
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    # ... does work, saves files, prints status
```

### Registration in `patch_saving_functions` (line 2667):
```python
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

### Package structure
- `unsloth/__init__.py` line 263: `from .save import *` (so __all__ in save.py controls exports)
- `unsloth/kernels/utils.py:190-257`: `get_lora_parameters()` and `get_lora_parameters_bias()` 

## Requirements
1. Add an `export_to_onnx` method that:
   - Extracts LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) from the model
   - Exports them as ONNX format
   - Saves to a specified output directory
   - Includes metadata: rank, alpha, target modules
   - Accepts an `opset_version` parameter (default 17)
2. Expose it alongside existing save/export methods (register via `patch_saving_functions`)
3. Do NOT try to run or install unsloth

## Design questions to address:
1. How should the ONNX graph be structured? One ONNX model per LoRA module, or one ONNX model containing all adapters?
2. How to store metadata (rank, alpha, target_modules) in the ONNX file? (ONNX supports model metadata_props)
3. Should we also save a JSON config file alongside?
4. What should the function signature look like to be consistent with existing methods?

Please provide a detailed implementation plan with code structure, function signatures, and the exact locations in files where changes need to be made.

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass
